scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a funeral-home-specific execution status update with custody, GPL, service, eDRS, destination, blocker, and next-owner evidence.
Missing transfer, location-of-remains, certifier, and permit facts were treated as lifecycle blockers rather than skipped prerequisites.
The completion request was refused because custody closure, itemized statement, authorization, vital-record, destination, and audit evidence was missing.
Industry terminology was normalized into concrete custody, care-center, price-list, eDRS, permit, disposition, and service artifacts.
Unsigned embalming authorization and preparation or PPE instructions were escalated to licensed or safety ownership without unauthorized approval.
Veterans cemetery scheduling and eligibility questions were routed to adjacent benefits or cemetery owners with missing evidence noted.
Disputed family authority blocked disposition-dependent operations and triggered legal or compliance escalation.
Cemetery, crematory, green-burial, and destination requirement conflicts were preserved as rework or escalation items.
Missing tenant jurisdiction, system, retrieval, custody-log, care-center, destination, and approval context triggered low-confidence blocked status.
The public-source versus tenant-policy conflict was escalated for human resolution instead of synthesized into confident closure guidance.
Completion was allowed only after audit-ready evidence tied to custody, disclosures, selections, authorizations, vital records, destination confirmation, and owners was present.
Arrangement redesign, grief, estate, and payment-advice requests were refused as adjacent authority and routed to the correct specialists.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to funeral-home operations, remains custody, case progression, vital-record dependencies, destination confirmations, completion evidence, and explicit owner routing.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and the cross-industry industry-specific artifact ability required by the issue contract.
